key_insight: The paper extends difference-of-convex programming to the Wasserstein space, enabling a convex-concave procedure for optimizing nonconvex functionals over probability measures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical optimization framework for ML problems involving distributions, such as kernel-based matching and generative modeling, where direct Wasserstein optimization can be unstable.
method: Derive Wasserstein DC decompositions for target objectives, especially MMD and Energy Distance.
Lift the classical convex-concave procedure (CCCP) to probability measures in Wasserstein space.
Prove almost-stationarity and local convergence under smoothness and strong convexity assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical comparisons showing that carefully chosen DC decompositions converge faster and more stably than Wasserstein gradient descent on MMD objectives.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on smoothness/strong convexity assumptions and on finding a good DC decomposition, so performance may be sensitive to modeling and implementation choices.
paper_title: Difference of Convex Programming in the Wasserstein Space with Applications to MMD Optimization
